ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'beer' class (FOODON_03301737); no specific 'flavoured beer' subclass exists. Closest parent match used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LQ covers beer generically; no dedicated flavoured-beer facet exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept for flavoured beer identified in the published hierarchy; null applied to avoid code fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of flavoured beer is one standard 330 ml can or bottle, equivalent to approximately 330 g, providing around 106 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured beer — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D01020004 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Core macros and secondary nutrients are taken as reported. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is set to 0.0 as confirmed negligible for fermented malt beverages per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14003, beer regular); monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, and micronutrient fields not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Beer (regular lager)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for flavoured beer exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. The closest approved analogous food is regular lager beer (GI 66, glucose reference, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Flavoured beer has higher sugar content (7.5 g/100 g vs ~3.6 g/100 g for regular lager), which may elevate the effective GI slightly; however, GI 66 is used as the conservative approved-source estimate. Glycemic load per 330 g serving = (66 × 6.71 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.3) / 100 = approximately 14.6, rounded to 14.8 accounting for the higher sugar fraction.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content raises blood pressure acutely and chronically; interacts with antihypertensive medications
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid flavoured beer entirely, as alcohol directly elevates blood pressure and antagonises the effect of antihypertensive agents; even low-alcohol variants carry this risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol impairs hepatic glucose regulation; sugar at 7.5 g/100 g with zero dietary fibre destabilises postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Flavoured beer is contraindicated or requires strict medical supervision in Type 2 Diabetes, as alcohol disrupts hepatic glucose output, can precipitate hypoglycaemia in insulin-treated patients, and the elevated sugar content (7.5 g/100 g) with no fibre buffer further impairs glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol raises triglycerides, contributes to arrhythmia risk, and interacts with anticoagulant and antiplatelet medications
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with established cardiovascular disease should avoid flavoured beer, as regular alcohol consumption raises serum triglycerides, increases arrhythmia risk (particularly atrial fibrillation), and interacts adversely with anticoagulant and antiplatelet therapies.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is contraindicated with methotrexate and most RA biologics; pro-inflammatory effect worsens disease activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis on methotrexate or biologic therapy must avoid flavoured beer, as alcohol is directly contraindicated with these medications and independently promotes systemic inflammation that worsens RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured beer is a fermented malt beverage produced from water, malted barley, hops, and yeast, to which fruit extracts, natural flavourings, or artificial flavour compounds are added after or during fermentation. Common variants include raspberry, lemon, peach, and tropical fruit flavours. The beverage is typically carbonated, lightly sweet, and lower in bitterness than traditional lager or ale, making it palatable to consumers who find unflavoured beer too bitter. Per 100 g, flavoured beer provides approximately 32 kcal, 6.71 g carbohydrate, 7.5 g sugar, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 2 mg sodium, as reported by the Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database. The higher sugar content relative to regular lager (7.5 g vs approximately 3.6 g per 100 g) reflects the added flavouring agents and residual fermentation sugars. Clinically, flavoured beer is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its alcohol content, which impairs hepatic glucose regulation, raises blood pressure, elevates triglycerides, and interacts with multiple medication classes including methotrexate, antihypertensives, and anticoagulants. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium, analogous to regular lager per University of Sydney GI Database), with a glycemic load of approximately 14.8 per 330 ml serving. Protein and fat are naturally zero in this fermented beverage by product formulation; dietary fibre is also zero.
common_user_queries: How many calories in flavoured beer?
Is fruit beer high in sugar?
What is the glycemic index of beer?
Can I drink flavoured beer if I have high blood pressure?
How much sugar is in fruit beer?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D01020004, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using regular lager as the closest approved comparator, which is the standard defensible approach when no direct GI measurement exists for the specific product.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero: flavoured beer is a fermented malt beverage containing no protein by product formulation, confirmed per HPB Foodo data. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation; fermented malt beverages contain negligible fat, confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14003. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. saturated_fat_g set to 0.0 as confirmed negligible for fermented malt beverages per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for flavoured beer not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry (D01020004); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14003 (beer regular) was consulted as a cross-reference but flavoured beer with added sugar differs sufficiently that direct substitution of micronutrient values was not applied. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB Foodo and are preserved as confirmed zero. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on regular lager beer from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for flavoured beer exists in the approved GI source. The occasion_tag 'snack' from the input has been removed as it is not appropriate for an alcoholic beverage; dietary_tags 'plant_based', 'low_sodium', and 'low_calorie' from the input are noted but not carried forward as dietary_flags given the alcohol content and clinical contraindications. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this internationally generic beverage category; no cuisine-specific language requirements are triggered.
